website.text_excerpt: OpenHuman — TinyHumans TinyHumans OpenHuman Your Personal AI super intelligence. Private, Simple and extremely powerful. Download Documentation GitHub One Subscription. That's it. One subscription gives you access to 30+ providers needed to build your AI assistant. No more juggling multiple subscriptions. Incredible Memory OpenHuman remembers upto 1 billion tokens of memory. Which means you can give it everything about yourself and it'll know everything about you. Get Started in Minutes Once you connect your world, OpenHuman can start helping immediately. You do not have to wait days for your AI to learn your context. Personalized Learning OpenHuman learns from you. This includes information from your screen, text, emails etc.. All kept privately. Runs a Local AI Model A local LLM handles low-level tasks like summarizing, tooling, etc so that your privacy stays off the cloud. Simple or Advanced Connect tools like Gmail/Notion etc in a few clicks for fast setup or configure credentials manually for maximum control. TinyHumans Your Personal AI super intelligence. items.tagline: A unified storage SDK for object and blob backends
items.description: A unified storage SDK for object and blob backends

website.title: Files SDK - One API for S3, R2, GCS, Azure & blob storage
website.description: A unified storage SDK for object and blob backends. One small, honest API. Web-standards I/O. An escape hatch when you need the native client.
website.text_excerpt: Files SDK - One API for S3, R2, GCS, Azure & blob storage Home Adapters AI CLI API Updates haydenbleasel/files-sdk Files SDK A unified storage SDK for object and blob backends. One small, honest API. Web-standards I/O. An escape hatch when you need the native client. + 26 more On this page S3 R2 Vercel Blob Netlify Blobs MinIO import { Files } from " files-sdk " ; import { s3 } from " files-sdk/s3 " ; const files = new Files ({ adapter : s3 ({ bucket : " uploads " , region : " us-east-1 " }), }); await files . upload ( " hello.txt " , " world " ); const file = await files . download ( " hello.txt " ); const meta = await files . head ( " hello.txt " ); const items = await files . list (); await files . delete ( " hello.txt " ); Why Object storage SDKs are all subtly different. files-sdk exposes the slice that's the same everywhere - upload, download, list, delete - behind a single class, and gets out of the way for anything provider-specific. One small API across providers. Swap your storage provider without rewriting calls. Web-standards I/O. Accepts File , Blob , ReadableStream , ArrayBuffer , string . Runs on Node, Bun, Workers, Vercel - anywhere fetch runs. Escape hatch via files.raw . The native client is always one property away, typed per adapter - versioning, lifecycle, ACLs, multipart, all of it. Predictable errors. A single FilesError with a normalized code across providers, and the original error attached as cause . Installation npm pnpm bun yarn npm install files-sdk Each provider's native SDK is an optional peer dependency - install only the ones you actually use, alongside files-sdk itself. See the adapter you're using for its install command. If you import an adapter without its peer installed, Node throws ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND naming the missing package....

Swift SwiftUI SwiftData SwiftTerm Download for Mac Overview Marketplace Submit a plugin FAQ Feedback Multi Sessions. Run multiple sessions per project in tabs, and switch between projects in the sidebar — each gets its own Claude Code instance with persistent history. Multi-Account Switching. Configure multiple Claude accounts. Claudy auto-switches when one approaches its usage limit, with real-time usage bars for each account. Compact Mode. Shrink the window and Claudy adapts — the sidebar collapses and a quick-switch bar appears so you stay focused without the clutter. Draft Commits. Checkpoint work mid-session as [Draft] git commits. AI-labels each one, lets you restore any checkpoint, then squash into a clean final commit. Marketplace. Browse and install MCP servers, skills, and slash commands from the built-in marketplace.
